title: Rustic Ukrainian Dough Dumplings
description: Yeasty, tender dumplings with a silky kinako filling, perfect for a cozy Ukrainian lunch or dinner
instructions: In a small bowl combine flour, yeast, sugar, and salt.
Add warm water and olive oil to the dry mixture and stir until a sticky dough forms.
Transfer dough onto a floured surface and knead for 8 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour until doubled in size.
In the meantime, mix kinako, grated carrot, dill, and lemon zest in a bowl for the filling.
Dough has risen, punch it down, and roll it into a long log about 1 inch thick.
Cut the log into 1.5 inch pieces and flatten each piece into a round disc with a rolling pin.
Spoon a tablespoon of filling onto each disc, fold over, and seal the edges by pinching them together.
Heat a large pot of vegetable broth to a simmer and place dumplings in the pot, cooking for 3-4 minutes until they float to the surface.
Remove dumplings with a slotted spoon and serve hot, optionally drizzled with a little olive oil or a squeeze of lemon.
Optionally reduce remaining broth by simmering it for 10 minutes to serve as a light sauce.
